title: Neuroscience and cognition
explanation:
Neuroscience and cognition

  • neuron, cortex, cognition, perception, stimulus
  • memory, neural, synapse, consciousness, impairment
  • verbs: process, retain, stimulate, perceive

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: Information is ____ in different regions of the brain. | *processed | printed | promoted
Damage to the frontal ____ can affect decision-making. | *cortex | context | contract
Repeated ____ strengthens certain neural pathways. | *stimulation | station | stipulation
Mild cognitive ____ may affect short-term recall. | *impairment | improvement | implement

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option. Use each option ONLY ONCE.

questions.text: A ____ transmits signals between nerve cells. | synapse
The experiment measured reaction to a visual ____.  | stimulus
Sleep helps the brain ____ important memories. | retain
Patients reported altered ____ after the injury. | perception
Scientists study how ____ emerges from brain activity. | consciousness

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: Each ____ connects to thousands of others. | *neuron | notion | nation
____ decline is common in advanced age. | *Memory | Memorial | Memorise
The study focuses on higher-order ____.  | *cognition | cognitive | cognise
Brain scans reveal ____ activity during tasks. | *neural | neutral | natural
